title: ClickHouse Mutation and Lightweight Delete Backlog
app: Splunk DB Connect or scripted poll of system.mutations
spl:
index=database sourcetype="clickhouse:mutations"
| where is_done==0 OR is_done=="false"
| where parts_to_do > 100
| stats max(parts_to_do) as backlog latest(create_time) as started by database, table, mutation_id, host
| sort -backlog
description: Large parts_to_do counts mean ALTER UPDATE/DELETE mutations are not finishing—common after bulk changes or schema migrations on MergeTree tables.
value: Avoids surprise query slowdowns and replication delay when mutations pile up across shards.
implementation: Ingest mutations every few minutes; normalize is_done as boolean. Alert when any open mutation exceeds parts_to_do policy. Correlate with disk IO and merges. Cancel or throttle mutations per runbook.
visualization: Table (table, mutation_id, backlog), Line chart (parts_to_do), Single value (open mutations).
knownFalsePositives: Planned failovers, network maintenance, or heavy bulk replication can extend lag for a time without an outage; align the alert with the DR runbook and change window.
grandmaExplanation: We log unexpected schema and object changes so we can tie database drift to a change ticket or an investigation when something does not look intentional.
